type: content/directory micro-site — paid ($49 LTD) database of AI-agent ideas (Next.js 15/Supabase/Stripe/Cloudflare)
asking_price: $2,000 (TrustMRR; '0.1x' = last-30d spike annualized — honest: ~0.82x all-time / ~2.1x true ARR)
one_line_reason: First in-budget deal ($2k vs $3k) but a verified spike-and-flip: a 3-week-old domain sold as a 14-month business (whois 2026-06-27 vs claimed May-2025 founding), the $1,422 'spike month' is ~29 x $49 lifetime deals with $0 in the last 7 days, the '$78 MRR / 19 subs' cannot come from the site's own no-subscription pricing, the live site is fronted by a DIFFERENT person (@iamfra5er) than the seller (@mattmerrick16), and the only acquisition channel is the seller's personal X audience - which does not convey.

operability_upside.note: Next.js 15/Supabase/Stripe one-pager - trivially operable; but it's a content template, not a product: upside requires audience-building, not engineering.

pmf_retention.note: No recurring product exists: site sells a $49 one-time lifetime deal ('No subscriptions'); the spike month (~29 LTD sales) came from an audience blast, $0 in last 7d; the '19 subs/$78 MRR' cannot come from this pricing page (provenance unknown, MRR -45%/30d).

unit_economics.note: LTD-only economics: revenue already collected by seller, buyer inherits lifetime-access + future-update obligations to ~29-50 customers with $0 attached; 95% margin is meaningless with no recurring engine.

moat_defensibility.note: 3-week-old template site, DR 0, 1 page indexed, gated unverifiable (plausibly AI-generated) content; free substitutes everywhere (GitHub lists, IdeaBrowser at scale, ChatGPT).

growth_market.note: AI-agent interest is a genuine tailwind, but this slice (paid idea lists) is brand/audience-attached; without a distribution engine there is no market access.

tech_ip.note: Modern minimal stack transfers easily; content provenance unverified; the live site's operator-of-record ambiguity (@iamfra5er) clouds what IP is actually conveyed.

transfer_key_person.note: The only channel is the seller's (or Fraser's) personal X audience; zero SEO, no email capture, no organic loop - the growth engine walks away at closing. Site literally tells customers to contact a different person than the seller.

deal_terms.note: $2,000 = ~2.1x a declining true ARR ($936) or ~1.4x a one-time spike month; category comps for sub-$100-MRR directories clear at $50-500; the 0.1x headline is spike-annualization. Plus inherited LTD obligations.

seller_adverse_selection.note: Verified worst-case: serial flipper (runs under2k.net, a flip marketplace; 10 near-$0 projects; 2 simultaneous listings) flipping days after an LTD spike, 'need the funds fast', fake scarcity countdown, domain 3 weeks old sold as 14 months, seller is not the site's public operator.

key_unknowns: Who actually owns/operates the site today - the footer says @iamfra5er (Fraser), the seller is @mattmerrick16, and whois updated the day after listing; if already sold/encumbered the listing is stale (deal-breaker).
Provenance of the '$78 MRR / 19 subscriptions' - the site sells no subscription; if from another product on the same Stripe account, the listed metrics misattribute revenue (confirms 'contradicted').
What the claimed May-2025 founding refers to, given the domain was registered 2026-06-27 - is any of the $2,431 all-time revenue attributable to THIS asset?
Content provenance: are the '257 curated ideas / 25+ guides' original work or AI-generated filler (affects the only real asset being sold).
Whether the ~29 LTD buyers' lifetime obligations (access + 'all future updates') transfer contractually to the buyer.
seller_questions: [deal-breaker | transfer] The live site's contact is @iamfra5er, not you - who owns and operates agentideahub.com today, and what exactly transfers (domain, code, Stripe customers, content)? Why did whois update on July 9?

[deal-breaker | revenue] The domain was registered 2026-06-27 but the listing claims founded May 2025 with $2,431 all-time - walk me through which revenue was earned by THIS product on THIS domain, with Stripe product-level export.

[deal-breaker | revenue] The site sells only a $49 lifetime deal ('No subscriptions') - what are the '19 active subscriptions / $78 MRR' on the listing? Which product do they belong to and do they transfer?
[pmf] Channel attribution for the ~29 spike-month sales: what % came from your X audience / Fraser's audience vs any organic source? Any analytics access?
[unit econ] What obligations do the lifetime buyers hold ('all future updates')? Provide the exact terms shown at purchase.
[deal terms] Given $0 revenue in the last 7 days and -45% MRR, what does the $2,000 price buy beyond the template and <=50 emails?
inputs_provided: TrustMRR .md profile figures (MRR $78, 19 subs, all-time $2,431, last-30d $1,422, last-7d $0, -45.1% 30d, margin 95%, ask $2,000, 11 offers, listed 2026-07-08, founded 2025-05-01, DR 0) - marketplace_attested, cross-checked vs live page (VERIFIED consistent) and MCP API (100x cents-rendering bug, ratios identical)

agentideahub.com live fetch (2026-07-17): $49 lifetime-only pricing, 'No subscriptions', '7 spots left' static scarcity, footer contact @iamfra5er, Next.js 15/Supabase/Cloudflare, GA G-3VPN6WKVF5 - VERIFIED
whois: domain created 2026-06-27, updated 2026-07-09; zero Wayback captures; 1 page in Google index - VERIFIED
Spike arithmetic: 29 x $49 = $1,421 = last-30d $1,422 - ASSUMED (exact-fit inference)
Seller profile: 10 TrustMRR projects near-$0, runs under2k.net flip marketplace, second live listing (AI Brand Kits), Niche Tools marked sold - VERIFIED

sources.title: Agent Idea Hub live site ($49 LTD, @iamfra5er footer)
Matt Merrick Indie Page (portfolio; Nichetools sold)
Medium - 'I built and sold a project in 8 hours' (seller)
